fc_mcu_landscape.summary: STM32 (Swiss/EU) remains dominant FC MCU. AT32 (Chinese) is the low-cost challenger.

mcus.name: STM32F405
mcus.status: Workhorse FC MCU. Most F4 flight controllers use this.

mcus.name: STM32F722
mcus.status: Mid-range F7 FC. Better performance than F405.

mcus.name: STM32H743
mcus.status: Top-tier H7 FC. Required for some advanced Betaflight/ArduPilot features.

mcus.name: AT32F435
mcus.status: Chinese low-cost alternative to STM32F405. Gaining adoption rapidly. 30-50% cheaper.
mcus.strategic_note: AT32 adoption in FCs increases Chinese silicon dependency. Even 'NDAA-compliant' US brands may use AT32 to cut costs.

esc_mcu_landscape.summary: ESC MCUs are split between STM32 (allied) and AT32/GD32 (Chinese). AT32F421 is now the most popular ESC MCU for cost.

mcus.name: STM32G071
mcus.application: AM32 ESCs — best cost-performance for allied MCU

mcus.name: AT32F421
mcus.application: AM32 and BLHeli_32 ESCs. Most popular ESC MCU for new designs due to cost.
mcus.strategic_note: AT32F421 is deeply embedded in ESC supply chain. Hobbywing G2 series uses this chip.

mcus.name: GD32E230
mcus.status: Less common than AT32F421 but supported

esc_firmware_ecosystem.name: BLHeli_32
esc_firmware_ecosystem.license: Commercial — per-unit licensing fee to manufacturers
esc_firmware_ecosystem.market_share_note: Declining. Betaflight officially recommends AM32 over BLHeli_32.
esc_firmware_ecosystem.issue: Bugs reported in versions after 32.7. Betaflight warns against newer releases.
esc_firmware_ecosystem.strategic_note: Closed-source firmware from individual developer. Licensing fee adds $0.50-1.00 per ESC. No source code audit possible.

esc_firmware_ecosystem.name: AM32
esc_firmware_ecosystem.strategic_note: Open-source enables auditing and allied MCU support (STM32G071). Best path to NDAA-compliant ESC firmware.

esc_firmware_ecosystem.name: BLHeli_S / Bluejay
esc_firmware_ecosystem.market_share_note: Legacy 8-bit. Budget ESCs. Bluejay fork is recommended.

mosfet_supply_chain.summary: MOSFETs are the power switching elements in ESCs. 6 per motor phase (2 per phase × 3 phases). Supply is diversified across US, European, and Chinese vendors.

key_vendors.name: Infineon Technologies
key_vendors.note: Premium power MOSFETs. Used in high-end ESCs.

key_vendors.name: Various Chinese
key_vendors.note: Budget ESCs use Chinese MOSFETs (unbranded or from Huawei HiSilicon-adjacent fabs). Identified by lack of datasheet/marking on cheaper ESCs.

mosfet_supply_chain.ndaa_status: MOSFET supply is well-diversified across allied nations. Not a chokepoint for NDAA compliance.

imu_sensors.summary: Inertial Measurement Units (IMUs) are critical FC components. Two main vendors.

vendors.name: Bosch Sensortec
vendors.status: BMI270 is the current standard Betaflight IMU. Most F4/F7/H7 FCs use this.

vendors.name: InvenSense (TDK)
vendors.ndaa_note: TDK is Japanese parent. InvenSense acquired 2017. MEMS fab in US/Japan.
vendors.status: ICM-42688-P used in premium FCs. MPU6000/6050 legacy.
vendors.note: Same TDK corporate family as ATL/Dongguan Poweramp (battery supplier), but InvenSense division is US-based.

imu_sensors.ndaa_status: IMU supply is NDAA-compliant. Both major vendors are allied (German/US-Japanese).

ndaa_stack_gap.problem: 79% of stacks in Forge DB are Chinese-manufactured. US brands (NewBeeDrone, Lumenier) likely use Chinese CM for PCB assembly.
ndaa_stack_gap.fc_mcu: STM32 (Swiss) is NDAA-compliant. AT32 (Chinese) is not — growing adoption threatens compliance.
ndaa_stack_gap.esc_mcu: STM32G071 (Swiss) is NDAA option. AT32F421 (Chinese) is cheaper and gaining share.
ndaa_stack_gap.esc_firmware: AM32 (open-source) can run on STM32 — best NDAA path. BLHeli_32 (closed-source) cannot be audited.
ndaa_stack_gap.mosfets: Allied supply exists (Infineon, TI, onsemi). Not a chokepoint.
ndaa_stack_gap.imu: Allied supply exists (Bosch, InvenSense/TDK). Not a chokepoint.
ndaa_stack_gap.pcb_assembly: Virtually all PCBs assembled in China (Shenzhen). NDAA compliance requires US/allied PCBA — very limited capacity.
ndaa_stack_gap.realistic_ndaa_stack: STM32 FC MCU + STM32 ESC MCU + AM32 firmware + Bosch/InvenSense IMU + allied MOSFETs + US/allied PCBA. Cost premium: 2-4x. Availability: very limited.
ndaa_stack_gap.best_hope: Orqa (Croatia) claims full in-house manufacturing. Ukrainian 603700 stacks are combat-proven and NDAA-adjacent.
